why_now: M1 is already green and TMu is present on master, so the next lowest unfinished blocker is making recursive types usable in elaborated/runtime terms.

acceptance_criteria: ElabTerm and ElabTermF gain ERoll ElabType ElabTerm and EUnroll ElabTerm, and all exhaustive elaborated-term consumers compile warning-free after adding conservative cases, including runtime helpers and test-side traversals.

Phase 7 typechecking accepts roll[μa. τ] e only when the annotation is a recursive TMu type and e has the unfolded payload type τ[μa.τ/a], and it accepts unroll e only when e has recursive type μa. τ and returns the same unfolded payload type.
Phase 7 typechecking rejects deterministic error cases for non-recursive roll annotations, non-recursive unroll inputs, and mismatched roll payloads.

Reduction is call-by-value and deterministic for the new forms: ERoll steps its payload until value, ERoll μ v is a value when v is a value, EUnroll steps its scrutinee until value, and EUnroll (ERoll μ v) reduces in one step to v.

Focused regressions are added to existing TypeCheckSpec and ReduceSpec for positive typing, negative typing, and unroll-roll reduction, and existing Phase 7 soundness/progress tests still pass after the constructor fallout updates.
